title: Animation Timeline Director
description: The user acts as a film director controlling an antique camera rig that films the InterpolationTest model performing its built-in animations. Users manipulate a multi-track timeline to blend, sequence, and reverse animation clips while the antique camera orbits on a motorized dolly track, with full undo/redo support and energy conservation governing total animation speed across coupled tracks.
prompt:
Create a single HTML file using Three.js r170 (via importmap from cdn.jsdelivr.net) that loads both InterpolationTest.glb and AntiqueCamera.glb using GLTFLoader and implements an Animation Timeline Director.

SCENE SETUP: Place the InterpolationTest model at the origin on a reflective circular stage. Position the AntiqueCamera model on a visible circular dolly track (torus geometry, radius 5) surrounding the stage. Add a spotlight attached to the camera model and soft ambient light. Use a perspective camera that defaults to a top-down 3/4 view.

ANIMATION SYSTEM: Extract all AnimationClips from InterpolationTest. Display an HTML overlay timeline panel at the bottom with one horizontal track per clip. Each track shows a draggable colored bar representing that clip's active time window. Users can: (1) drag bars left/right to offset clip start times, (2) drag bar edges to trim duration, (3) click a bar to select it and use a speed slider (0.1x-3.0x), (4) toggle reverse playback per clip via a button. CONSERVATION LAW: the sum of all clip speeds multiplied by their durations must remain constant (total animation energy = 10). Adjusting one clip's speed auto-scales others proportionally.

CAMERA DOLLY: A separate track controls the AntiqueCamera's position along the dolly circle (0-360 degrees). Users drag a dolly keyframe marker to set camera angle. The spotlight follows camera orientation. The main viewport renders from a fixed overview, but a small picture-in-picture panel (top-right, 320x240) renders the scene from the AntiqueCamera's perspective using a second WebGLRenderer or scissor rendering.

INTERACTIONS (10-14 steps): Drag clip bars to reorder timing, resize clips, adjust speed sliders, toggle reverse on multiple clips, reposition dolly camera, play/pause the full timeline, and use Ctrl+Z/Ctrl+Y for undo/redo across all operations. Maintain a history stack of at least 20 states. Add a master Play/Pause button and a timeline scrub handle. Display current total energy value to verify conservation. All UI uses clean HTML/CSS overlays.

[State Exposure (required)]
Expose window.__3D_STATE__ updated every animation frame:
window.__3D_STATE__ = {
  cameraOrbitAngle: number,         // current angle in radians of the antique camera on the dolly track (0 to 2π)
  cameraOrbitSpeed: number,         // rotation speed of camera dolly in radians per second
  activeClipCount: number,         // how many animation clips are currently playing (weight > 0)
  totalAnimationEnergy: number,         // conservation constant, sum of (clipSpeed × clipDuration) across all clips, fixed at 10
  selectedClipIndex: number,         // index of currently selected clip in timeline (-1 if none selected)
  spotlightIntensity: number,         // intensity of the spotlight attached to the camera model
  playbackMasterSpeed: number,         // global time scale multiplier for all animations (0.1 to 3.0)
  isTimelinePlaying: boolean,         // whether the timeline is actively advancing or paused
  dollyTrackRadius: number,         // radius of the circular dolly track in world units
  stageReflectivity: number,         // reflectivity coefficient of the circular stage floor (0.0 to 1.0)
  clipReverseFlags: string,         // comma-separated binary flags indicating reverse state of each clip (e.g., '0,1,0,1')
  totalClipOffsetSum: number,         // sum of all clip start time offsets in seconds
}
